title: React DevTools Integration
overview: Add React DevTools integration — component tree, suspense tracking, props inspection, render profiling. Lazy-downloaded hook injected via addInitScript.
scopeChallenge: next-browser implements this pattern. We adapt: lazy download instead of vendoring, CLI commands instead of programmatic API. Core logic (hook injection + evaluate queries) is identical.

failureModes.mitigation: Cache permanently. Clear error with manual install instructions.

failureModes.risk: Hook not injected before page load
failureModes.mitigation: Lazy mode tells user to reload. BROWSE_REACT_DEVTOOLS=1 avoids this.
Check hook existence. Return clear message.
Tree/suspense still work. Profiler needs profiling build. Document.

tasks.title: React DevTools module (lazy download + hook + queries)
tasks.description: Create src/react-devtools.ts with ensureHook(), injectHook(), getTree(), getProps(), getSuspense(), getProfiler().
acceptanceCriteria: getTree returns component tree on React pages
getTree returns clear message on non-React pages

tasks.title: React meta commands
tasks.description: Add react-tree, react-props, react-suspense, react-profiler to meta.ts and META_COMMANDS.

tasks.title: Auto-inject hook on context creation
tasks.description: BROWSE_REACT_DEVTOOLS=1 auto-injects hook. Without flag, lazy injection on first react-* command with reload hint.
acceptanceCriteria: Without flag, lazy injection with reload hint

tasks.title: Integration tests
tasks.description: Test React DevTools commands against a simple React page fixture.
acceptanceCriteria: react-tree on React page returns component names
react-tree on non-React page returns No React detected

tasks.title: Documentation
tasks.description: Update SKILL.md, README, CHANGELOG with React DevTools commands.
acceptanceCriteria: When to Use What table updated
